The fact that having a negative checking account balance exerts a positive impact on default probability, because it increases the likelihood of default, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [1]
The fact that the loan duration being shorter increases the likelihood of credit risk, because it extends repayment exposure, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [2]
The fact that not having a checking account is associated with a lower estimated risk, because it correlates with stable financial profiles, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [3]
